situational_context: Extended low-training-load rebuild continues: chronic load at 18.1 (~1.1% of 2023 historical peak of 1608). Activity dominated by e-bike commuting on 'sur site' days (load 8-12/leg) with one Zwift session last week (load 15). Readiness trending positively: RHR down to 51.9 (from 53), training readiness 95, body battery 57 (7d best). HRV stable at 36 weekly avg (within 90d baseline 35.4 +/- 3.0). Sleep averaging 7.3h with one sub-6h night. Weight 105.85 kg (down ~1 kg over 2 weeks). Yesterday's planned run skipped (no calendar block, silent skip). Three structured sessions planned Fri-Sun. Pattern consistent with life-constrained rebuild (infant cohabitant).

recommendations.text: Execute the 3 planned structured sessions this weekend (Fri Zwift, Sat outdoor ride, Sun recovery run) — consistency is the single most important rebuild lever, especially after yesterday's skipped session
recommendations.measurable: 3/3 planned sessions completed by Sunday evening

recommendations.text: Increase daily protein to >=150g — current 7d average is ~129g, well below target. Insufficient protein during rebuild limits muscular adaptation
recommendations.measurable: >=150g protein logged on >=5 of next 7 days

recommendations.text: Log a Withings Body Scan this weekend to track body composition alongside the training rebuild. Last scan was Apr 18 (105.85 kg, no composition detail)
recommendations.measurable: >=1 full Body Scan with fat%/muscle mass logged by Apr 27

markers_to_watch.threshold: Sustaining >=35 ms confirms stable recovery capacity
markers_to_watch.reason: Currently at 36 (within 90d baseline). Watch for drops below 33 (>1 sigma below baseline) as training load increases

markers_to_watch.threshold: Target gradual increase toward 25-30 over next 2 weeks
markers_to_watch.reason: At 18.1 (1.1% of peak). Structured sessions should begin moving this if adherence improves
Yesterday's skip continues a pattern of difficulty converting plans to execution. Baby constraints expected, but monitoring calibrates planning
